sections.paragraph:
fut. יִלְחַם-

(1) to eat (kindred to לָהַם, see under the root לוּעַ ), i.q. אָכַל, but only used poetically, Pro 23:1 followed by an acc. of the food, Pro 4:17 23:6 followed by בְּ to eat of anything (an, von etwas effen), Pro 9:5 Psa 141:4. Metaph. Deu 32:24, לְחוּמֵי רֶשֶׁף “consumed with pestilence.”

(2) to fight, to war, followed by אֶת Psa 35:1 and לְ of pers. 56:2 , Psa 35:3more used in Niph. Fierce soldiers are hyperbolically said to devour their enemies, as Joshua says of the Canaanites. Num 14:9, לַחמֵנוּ הֵם “they shall be our bread;” Luth. denn wir wollen fie wie Brot freffen; compare Arab. مضغ to eat; Conj. II. to fight; Pers. مردن خور anthropophagus, spoken of a fierce soldier; compare also Hom. πτολέμοιο μέγα στόμα, Il. 10:8.

Niphal, נִלְחַם inf. absol. נִלְחֹם i.q. Kal No. 2, to fight, to wage war (with a recipr. signif. like the Gr. μάχεσθαι). 1Sa 17:10, נִלָּֽחֲמָה יַחַד “let us fight together;” followed by an acc. of one’s opponent, Jos 10:25 Psa 109:3 1Ki 20:25 followed by בְּ Exo 1:10 עִם 2Ki 13:12 14:15 אֶל Jer 1:19 15:20 עַל Neh 4:8 also followed by לְ Exo 14:14, 25 Exo 14:25 Deu 1:30 and by עַל of that for which one fights, Jud. 9:17 2Ki 10:3 נִלְחַם בָּעִיר Jud. 9:45 and עַל עִיר to fight against a city, to besiege it, Isa 7:1 2Ki 16:5 Jer 34:22 37:8 .

Derivatives לֶחֶם, לָחֵם, לְחוּם, מִלְחָמָה.
